new_classes.definition_en: A spirit manufactured using a special yeast from sugar-cane molasses or from molasses of the sweet juices of plants and of rice, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 (Chapter 22). Distinct from raki, which is obtained by redistilling raisin or dry fig spirits with aniseed.

new_classes.label_en: Arrack in Containers of 2 Litres or Less (CN 22089011)
new_classes.label_de: Arrak in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 Litern oder weniger (KN 22089011)
new_classes.definition_en: Arrack (a spirit produced from sugar-cane molasses or plant-juice molasses and rice using a special yeast) presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN code 22089011.

new_classes.label_en: Sugar-Cane Molasses and Rice Fermentation/Distillation Process (Arrack)
new_classes.definition_en: Process of fermenting and distilling sugar-cane molasses (or molasses from the sweet juices of plants) together with rice, using a special yeast, to produce arrack. Distinct from standard sugar-cane distillation used for rum.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the key distinguishing criteria for CN 22089011: (1) arrack as a spirit class with its specific production process (special yeast, sugar-cane molasses or plant-juice molasses, rice); (2) the small-container subclass (≤ 2 L) linked to code 22089011; (3) the production process class distinguishing arrack from rum/standard sugar-cane spirits. A small gap remains: the explicit negative criterion (not raki / not redistilled raisin or fig spirits with aniseed, falling under 22089056 or 22089090) is partially addressed via disjointness with RumAndSugarCaneSpirit but the raki distinction is not formally axiomatised because no Raki class exists in the TBox. The container-volume upper bound (≤ 2 L) discriminating 22089011 from 22089019 is captured via the decimalRange restriction.
